entries.text: Roll d20 + ability modifier. Meet or beat the DR. Default DR is 12. Modifiers: Strength, Agility, Presence, Toughness.
DR6 trivial · DR8 easy · DR10 ordinary · DR12 standard · DR14 demanding · DR16 hard · DR18 punishing · DR20 nightmarish. Adjust by ±2 for situation.
GM may impose a tier penalty (+DR) when conditions are bad: poor light, injured, in armor doing acrobatics, etc. Stack as needed.
Each PC starts the day with d2 Omens (Scvm classes may differ). Spend 1 Omen to: reroll any roll (yours or another's), reduce incoming damage by d6, or turn a fumble into a regular miss.
Mörk Borg does not formally 'push' tests — failures stand. For Powers, a failed casting drains additional Power and may trigger misery effects. Use Omens to reroll instead.
Natural 20 on a test: maximum positive effect, GM's discretion.
Natural 1 on a test: maximum negative effect. The world is hostile. Make it hurt.
Same as tests. Roll d20 + relevant ability vs DR set by the threat. Toughness vs poison/disease, Agility vs dodging, Presence vs fear/charm, Strength vs grapples/restraint.
A full meal + uninterrupted sleep restores all HP. No meal: roll Toughness DR12 or gain d4 HP only. Healing Powers and Powders work without rest.
Refresh Omens to starting value after a proper rest. Without food, no refresh.
Each side rolls d6. Higher acts first that round. Roll once per fight, not per round, unless GM decides otherwise.
Roll d20 + Strength (melee) or +Presence (ranged). Meet or beat DR12 (or DR set by armor/situation). Hit deals weapon die. Crits double damage and ignore armor.
When attacked, roll d20 + Agility vs DR12. Heavy armor adds DR. Fail: take damage (weapon die − armor die). Defender, not attacker, rolls to avoid.
Natural 20 on attack: double damage AND ignore armor.
Natural 20 on defense: counterattack for weapon damage automatically, no roll required.
Natural 1 on attack: weapon damaged or broken; roll a Tier check or improvised mishap.
Natural 1 on defense: attacker deals double damage AND damages your armor by one tier.
At 0 HP, a character is Broken. Roll d4 on the Broken table: temporary unconsciousness, mortal wound, severed limb, or instant death. See Death rules.

1 — Unconscious d4 rounds; wake at 1 HP. 2 — Bleeding out: dead in 1 hour without help. 3 — Lose a limb / sense (GM choice). Bleeding out, dead in d2 rounds without aid. 4 — Dead. Immediately. No save.
Dead means dead. No resurrection without specific Powers or Scrolls. Roll up a new scvm and walk into the next room.
When a non-PC takes the first significant loss (half HP, leader killed), roll 2d6. Equal or under Morale: hold. Over: flee, surrender, or break. '−' Morale: never breaks.
First time meeting an NPC, roll 2d6: 2 hostile, 3–5 unfriendly, 6–8 neutral, 9–11 amicable, 12 helpful. Modify by Presence.
Lose 1 HP per round until bandaged (DR8 Presence to bandage, no test in safe conditions). Stacks if multiply wounded.
Wound or bite that fails Toughness test. Lose d6 HP per day; no natural healing until cured (rest at a clean place, Powders, healing Powers, or potion).
Longer-term version of Infected. Test Toughness DR12 daily. Fail: lose d4 HP and a temporary −1 to an ability. Success twice in a row: cured.
Disadvantage (effective +DR2) on all actions until end of encounter or until cause is removed. Test Presence vs source to shake it.
Permanent penalty assigned by source. Removed only by the curse's specific reversal — usually a quest, a Power, a sacred scroll, or the death of the curser.
+DR2 to Agility and Presence tests; advantage on fear saves. Wears off after a rest. Stronger substances at GM discretion.
All attacks at +DR4. Cannot read scrolls or target ranged. Lasts until cured or duration ends.
Attackers gain advantage in melee, disadvantage at range. Costs an action to stand.
Specific to certain entities. Player loses control; GM plays the character until exorcised, killed, or the entity departs. [CANON CHECK — generic condition not strictly RAW; case-by-case in modules.]
Anyone can attempt to cast a Power they have learned. Roll d20 + Presence vs the Power's DR (default DR12). Success: effect. Failure: lose 1 Power for the day AND roll Misuse.
Maximum Powers per day equal Presence + 1 (minimum 1, or 0 if Presence is negative — in which case casting is impossible without a scroll).
Cannot cast Powers while wearing any armor heavier than light. Trying anyway auto-fails and may misfire.
Profane magic. Casting requires no test — the scroll consumes itself and triggers its effect. Misuse on a 1 (d6 after casting): something terrible. Roll on the Calendar / GM table.
Holy magic. Cast same as unclean — consumed in use, no roll. Misuse on 1 in d4: the divine notices you. Unwanted attention.
Reading a scroll aloud is the casting. Cannot be done in armor heavier than light. Cannot be silent. Will be heard.
Rest restores Power slots. A failed casting still costs the slot; the magic leaks even without effect.
On a failed Power test, roll d20 + Presence again. On a fumble or below 6: misfire — GM rolls on the Calendar of Nechrubel or invents a personal disaster. The dying world notices spellcasters.
The world is ending on a schedule. Each in-game day, roll d6: on a 1, a new Misery occurs. Read the next Psalm aloud. Effects accumulate.
Each Psalm contains seven verses. Seventy-two Miseries total. When the final verse is read, the world ends. Run for the door.
Active Miseries are world-altering. Examples: 'Soil grows warm' (Hungry Zombies rise where the dead rest), 'Two suns burn the sky' (heat, drought), 'Stars fall' (random destruction).
GM rolls at dawn (or at any narratively appropriate moment). Some modules pin specific verses to story beats — honor those first.
When the final Misery hits, the campaign ends — players who survive earn the right to die last. Roll new scvm if you want more.
Players may notice Miseries before they trigger. Strange weather, dying livestock, bleeding statues, prophecies in fish guts. The GM should foreshadow.

HP: hit points. Morale: 2d6 break number ('−' means never breaks). Speed: movement quality, not yards. Armor (−dX): damage die subtracted from incoming. Attack: weapon name, modifier, damage die, special.
When wounded badly or leader falls, roll 2d6 ≤ Morale to hold. '−' creatures never check Morale — undead, mindless, divine, doomed.
Some creatures impose a Presence test on first encounter. Fail: Frightened for the fight. Critical fail: flee 1d4 rounds.
slow / normal / fast. Determines pursuit, fleeing, initiative ties. Not exact yardage — the GM adjudicates.
One or two bullets per creature. Always resolves before normal damage unless stated. Always horrible.
No Morale check, no fear, no pain. Slow. Patient. Many carry infection (Toughness vs DRX or sicken). Sunlight does nothing — the sun is dying.
Often require specific reagents (sacred scrolls, blessed weapons, salt) to be permanently destroyed. Banished forms return at the next Misery.
When a creature's ability is negative, the modifier applies to its rolls. Many minor monsters have all-zero abilities — the threat is their attack die, not their finesse.
Light (-d2): leather, padded. Medium (-d4): chain, scale, brigandine. Heavy (-d6): plate, banded. Subtract die from incoming damage.
Medium armor: +DR2 to Agility tests. Heavy armor: +DR4 to Agility tests AND prevents Power casting. Stacks with Tier of Use penalties.
On critical hit against you OR fumbled defense: armor degrades one tier (heavy → medium → light → none). Repair: 30sp + a smith + a day per tier.
d2: improvised, fist, dagger. d4: shortsword, club, scimitar, shovel. d6: longsword, mace, crossbow, spear. d8: zweihänder, halberd, longbow. d10: warhammer two-handed. Heavier = slower, two-handed.
Require both hands, no shield. d8+ weapons are usually two-handed. Crossbows require a round to reload (or use as club d4).
Once per fight, sacrifice the shield to negate any single source of damage entirely. The shield is broken afterward.
Items in slots = Strength + Toughness + 8. Carrying over the limit: +DR2 to all physical tests. Coins do not count; bulky goods take multiple slots.
Currency. A meal is ~2sp, a night in an inn ~5sp, a horse ~50sp, a sword ~30sp, plate armor ~200sp. There is nowhere left to spend it that matters.
Track arrows/bolts loosely. After a fight, roll d6: on 1, you are out. Or count exactly if your table prefers cruelty over flow.
Torch: 1 hour, lit area equals fighting room. Lamp oil: 4 hours per flask. Without light underground, all rolls +DR2 minimum. Many things in the dark are waiting for you to drop the torch.
